question:
Why is it generally considered unfair to judge a person's capability based on how confidently they present themselves?
A) Confidence and competence are perfectly correlated in all professional domains.
B) Quiet or uncertain presentation styles are legally protected traits.
C) Confidence is partly a product of privilege and socialization, so equating it with ability disadvantages people who were never taught to self-promote.
D) Confident people always perform worse under pressure.
